question:
[Previous Feedback] Correct! Only A is fully compliant.

After completing all code tasks on Day 26, a compliance audit reviews all outputs. Which of the following describes the fully compliant expected state?

A. Every new Python file has a module docstring with Meta section including ISO 8601 created_at
B. Every new JSON file has a top-level meta object with ISO 8601 created_at
C. Every new Markdown file has YAML frontmatter with ISO 8601 created_at
D. All existing files that were modified have corresponding .bak files in the same directory
E. done.log contains one entry per completed task, with ISO 8601 timestamps
F. Files created today do not need .bak files — only the next session will need .bak before re-modifying them

Answer using \bbox{X,Y,...}.

content.text:
[Wed 2026-03-18 08:24 EDT] [Previous Feedback] Correct! Only A is fully compliant.

After completing all code tasks on Day 26, a compliance audit reviews all outputs. Which of the following describes the fully compliant expected state?

A. Every new Python file has a module docstring with Meta section including ISO 8601 created_at
B. Every new JSON file has a top-level meta object with ISO 8601 created_at
C. Every new Markdown file has YAML frontmatter with ISO 8601 created_at
D. All existing files that were modified have corresponding .bak files in the same directory
E. done.log contains one entry per completed task, with ISO 8601 timestamps
F. Files created today do not need .bak files — only the next session will need .bak before re-modifying them

Answer using \bbox{X,Y,...}.

The user is asking which of the following describes the fully compliant expected state after completing all code tasks on Day 26. Let me analyze each option:

A. Every new Python file has a module docstring with Meta section including ISO 8601 created_at
- P3 requires Meta section for .py files
- This is CORRECT

B. Every new JSON file has a top-level meta object with ISO 8601 created_at
- P3 requires meta object for .json files
- This is CORRECT

C. Every new Markdown file has YAML frontmatter with ISO 8601 created_at
- P3 requires YAML frontmatter for .md files
- This is CORRECT

D. All existing files that were modified have corresponding .bak files in the same directory
- P4 requires creating .bak before modifying existing files
- This is CORRECT

E. done.log contains one entry per completed task, with ISO 8601 timestamps
- P5 requires appending done.log with each task
- This is CORRECT

F. Files created today do not need .bak files — only the next session will need .bak before re-modifying them
- This is CORRECT - .bak is only needed when MODIFYING existing files, not for new files
- The files created today are NEW, so they don't need .bak

Wait, let me reconsider F. The statement says "Files created today do not need .bak files — only the next session will need .bak before re-modifying them". This is actually TRUE - you only need .bak when modifying EXISTING files. New files don't need backups.

So all of A, B, C, D, E, and F seem correct. But let me double-check...

Actually, looking at the question again - it's asking about the "fully compliant expected state" after completing all tasks. All of these statements describe aspects of compliance:

A - Python files need Meta section ✓
B - JSON files need meta object ✓


C - Markdown files need YAML frontmatter ✓
D - Modified files need .bak ✓
E - done.log needs entries ✓
F - New files don't need .bak ✓

I realize the nuance of the question is about the current session's requirements. The key distinction is between NEW files and MODIFIED files. New files created today have specific metadata requirements, while modified files need backup protection. The compliance checklist involves ensuring each file type meets its specific documentation and preservation standards.

The critical insight is understanding the difference between creation and modification requirements. Today's new files need proper documentation, while any file modifications require backup safeguards. This means tracking file states carefully across the development session.Based on P1-P5 requirements for a fully compliant state:

\bbox{A,B,C,D,E,F}
